string.text: Log: \nThey say there's an entrance to an underground passage in the valley that leads straight to the sarcophagus... Maybe worth checking out.

Log: 
Important: 
Pick up a package from Seriy and pass it on to his brother.
At the Bar, by midnight, goes by Rutik. Ask Barkeep.

Log: 
Pass Kuznetsov greetings from Predybaylo. 
Word for word: told me to bow with respect. 
He'll let you through free of charge.

Log: 
Once the network is up, pass it along to everyone: 
Emission after 3 AM. 
Intel from Freedom members at the Barrier.
Log: \nRecipe from the Swamp Doctor: \nApply a "Jellyfish" (all properties zeroed out) to the soles of your feet overnight. Clears up fungus.
Log: \nDo not sell: \n"Titanium Kolobok" — that one's for Sultan.
Incoming: \nLyokha! The Guide is seriously something else! Get this — he wiped out a whole pack of dogs past the bridge! By himself!!! O_o

Outgoing: \nNah, they're exaggerating! There were three of them — the Guide and two other stalkers. Still cool though. I think that one guy was there too, you know, the decked-out one... What's his name... You know who I mean.
Incoming: \nNope.
Outgoing: \nHe came through our village not long ago with a Vintorez, said hi by the campfire, then took off real fast....
Incoming: \nAnd what's a Vintorez?

Outgoing: \nMan, you're such a greenhorn! It's a sniper rifle, an awesome one. Costs a ton, honestly, but I'm gonna save up and get one. Hey, why don't you move to our village? What are you doing sitting out there on the farm?
Incoming: \nNah, you've got military patrols around there. They don't come near here.
Outgoing: \nWho cares about the military? Sidorovich runs things here — everything's fine.
Incoming: \nNo, I'm good where I am.
Outgoing: \nSuit yourself.
Outgoing: \nKolyan, do you know anyone in Freedom?
Incoming: \nI do, but keep it between us. What do you need?
Outgoing: \nNeed to get in touch with Screw. Looking for small screwdrivers — Phillips and Torx. Really small, like watchmaker's ones.
Incoming: \nDo you really have to go all the way to the Warehouses for them? Nobody closer has any?

Outgoing: \nSeems not, I've asked around — already driven everyone crazy with these screwdrivers. And why go there in person? Stalkers make that run one way or another. Just send them along with whoever's heading that way.
Incoming: \nYou know how much they'll charge you for that? Anyway, if you really need it — I'll tell my guy, he'll message you and say it's from me.
Outgoing: \nGreat, thanks!

Incoming: \nVityek, we're in deep trouble, help us out! Borov's men have us surrounded in the Valley, near the factory. We won't fight back — too many of them, we'll surrender. Please find two "Little Stars" and bring them here. Tomorrow morning, be near the bulldozer by the little swamp. Hand the artifacts to their courier, and I'll square up with you later.
Outgoing: \nNo need to go looking. Khimik has two "Little Stars". I'll borrow them from him. But keep in mind — it goes on your tab.
Incoming: \nFine by me. Just keep it quiet, don't tell our guys. We've dug something up here, and we don't need the noise. ;)

Outgoing: \nNo idea what's going on, but I smell profit. :) Consider it done. Just don't do anything stupid out there — don't provoke them, or they'll shoot you on the spot. And then two "Little Stars" go to waste for nothing. :))
Incoming: \nThanks. That's it, don't message me again.
Incoming: \nSanyok, hey! Heard the news? Turns out Sakharov is growing watermelons on the bunker grounds!
Outgoing: \nWhat kind of nonsense is that? He's not growing any watermelons — I was just there yesterday.

Incoming: \nDid you go around the back of the bunker? Didn't think so. He's got a melon patch back there. Write to him, work something out, buy yourself a watermelon — because getting anything tasty from outside the perimeter is next to impossible.

Outgoing: \nI'm not so sure... I can't message him, but if I end up there any time soon — I'll check. But where did you even get the idea that he has a melon patch? Oh... wait, I get it, is this some kind of joke?
Incoming: \nNot a joke. Someone told me today, and they heard it from some Marked One apparently.
Outgoing: \nAnd you're going to listen to him... Word is, he's not all there upstairs. But fine, I'll find out.
Incoming: \nHey, Valyukha... Weren't you collecting something for Sidorovich?
Outgoing: \nHey. Yeah, I was. Why? Are you heading there? Maybe you could drop it off? I really can't get away right now...
Incoming: \nNo need to rush anymore. Word is he died last night.
Outgoing: \nWho?! Sidorovich?! Get out of here! No way... What did he die of?
Incoming: \nNo idea. I just got a message from a Cordon guy: "Sidorovich died last night, funeral — Monday at 10 AM in the Cordon."
Outgoing: \nWell, damn... So what now? Who do we bring loot to?
Incoming: \nNo idea, but someone will fill the spot eventually. Nature abhors a vacuum... and so on.

Outgoing: 
You're pulling my leg!.. I asked around. Look at this: 
To all! I am alive and well, and had no intention of dying. The joker has been identified and punished. Sidorovich 
Ha! See that?
Incoming: \nWow... Bastards, have they no shame...
Outgoing: \nSidorovich!?
Incoming: \nYeah?
Outgoing: \nWhen's your next mail run outside?
Incoming: \nDay after tomorrow. Then the 27th.
Outgoing: \nHow much?
Incoming: \nIs it a letter? Or a package?
Outgoing: \nNo, a letter. Back home.
Incoming: \nUsual rate. Bring it over — we'll work something out. If it's for the 27th, it'll be cheaper.
Outgoing: \nIf I hand it to Seriy at the Garbage today — he should come to you — will it go out the day after tomorrow?

Incoming: \nWhat am I, FedEx? How should I know... I'll do my best. Don't seal the letter, put the money inside. Keep in mind, I read all letters and, if necessary, cross out anything that doesn't need to go through. And don't stuff anything extra in the envelope besides the money. Only the letter makes it past the perimeter — everything else stays with me.
Outgoing: \nYeah, I know, I've sent one before.
Incoming: \nAlright then, bring it today and I'll do my best to get it out tomorrow morning.
Outgoing \nThanks!
Incoming: \nLyokha! We're heading to Agroprom tomorrow. The army boys have flare gun rockets — need to grab about ten... no, better make it twenty.
Outgoing: \nSure, I'll get right on that. What do you need rockets for?
Incoming: \nDuty threw us a little job. Voronin's birthday is coming up. So they want us to bring some in. For a fireworks show. I said yes. Coming with me? Pay is good.

Outgoing: \nSure, they pay well — and the military shoots well too... And not flare guns into the sky, mind you, but heavy caliber from their APCs. The whole thing stinks to me... You clearly just want to use this as an excuse to stock up — they don't need us and our flare guns any more than last year's snow...
Incoming: \nWe're not going there to steal! I've been in touch with a guy there — he'll trade anything you want for vodka, even a Topol.
Outgoing: \nNo, I'm not going. And I'm telling you not to either. They'll shoot you — and on top of that, they'll take the vodka.
Incoming: \nWhat a coward you are! Why did you even come to the Zone? Should've just stayed home...
Outgoing: \nNone of your business. And don't come to me with this kind of nonsense again.
Incoming: \nMityay, I hear you write poetry? Hey.
Outgoing: \nEvery now and then... Hey.
Incoming: \nI need something about the Zone, but for a girl, you know?

Outgoing: \nWhat's there to figure out? And where did you find yourself a girl? Maybe let me in on that too... Alright, here it is, short version: "The Zone, it's terrifying, death around every corner, but I love you and think only of you, even in my darkest hour." Will that do?
Incoming: \nYeah, something like that. How'd you come up with it so fast?

Outgoing: 
I literally just wrote something like that not long ago. Here, take a look — fresh off the press: 
We lit a fire by the village at night, 
The resin on the logs like a tear, 
I strummed a song by firelight 
About your smile and eyes so dear. 
You are so far away right now, 
Checkpoints stand between us two, 
The road to you is long somehow, 
As long as I've got bolts, I'll get through. 
That's just a piece of it. Will it work?
Incoming: \nYeah, exactly what I needed! Can I get the whole poem?
Outgoing: \nFour thousand or a "Flash".
Incoming: \nThat's highway robbery!
Outgoing: \nThen come up with your own!
Incoming \nAlright, fine, I'll think about it. I'll knock on your door again.
Incoming: \nSakharov is a rat!!!
Outgoing: \nHey. What's gotten into you? What did he do?

Incoming: \nHad a deal with the professor — he was going to give me a good job. I show up, and there's already some Duty member sitting there. Sakharov goes, sorry, pal, you're too late, already sorted. And they're both grinning. The Duty guy especially — smug mug on him!
Outgoing: \nWell, what did you expect? Plain and simple: first one in gets the job. Sakharov has a soft spot for Duty members.
Incoming: \nThat's not how you do business. We had an agreement!
Outgoing: \nAh, let it go. Come with me to the Garbage the day after tomorrow — Bes needs something: he's putting together a big group.
Incoming: \nGoing to take out bandits or something? Is Bes actually there? I thought I heard he'd left...

Outgoing: \nNah, probably not bandits — it's quiet there right now. But something's up... Does it matter whether it's Bes or not? We're sitting here broke. At least there's work, and people to shoot the breeze with.
Incoming: \nAll you ever want to do is shoot the breeze... Fine, let's go. First thing in the morning?
Outgoing: \nYeah. Are you at the Bar right now? I'll find you there tomorrow at eight. We'll hit the Garbage right around noon.
Incoming: \nDeal, I'll be waiting.
Incoming: \nHey! We need supplies. What's the situation like right now?
Outgoing: \nAnd same to you! Situation — not great. What exactly do you need?
Incoming: \nSomething non-perishable. Canned stew, mostly. Dry rations. Spirits.
Outgoing: \nHeading somewhere far?
Incoming: \nOh, three or four days or so. Five of us. Plus seven more at the site. Need supplies for them too.
Outgoing: \nNah, I'm nearly empty right now. Sidor's got a resupply coming tomorrow. Tonight, actually.
Incoming: \nThat's a long hike to get to him on foot.
Outgoing: \nThen don't go. Ask for delivery.
Incoming: \nToo pricey. And no guarantee the delivery makes it through. Heard the dogs are breeding again over at the Garbage?
Outgoing: \nKolyan, I can't help you no matter what. So — go to Sidor. Or you could try Bootlegger. :)
Incoming: \nHaha, very funny.
Outgoing \n:)) So — Sidor. No question.
Incoming \nThanks, real comforting.
Outgoing: \nIgoryosha, help me out!
Incoming: \nWhat is it now?
Outgoing: \nI need to go to ground urgently. Disappear for a couple of weeks.
Incoming: \nWell... what do you need me for?

Outgoing: \nLook, someone might come to you or one of your guys asking about me. They know I used to run loot with you. Tell them I headed toward Agroprom... Say I definitely went that way... Or better yet, throw in something like — some guy from Agroprom mentioned the dogs got to me, something along those lines...
Incoming: \nKnock on wood! Is it really that bad?
Outgoing: \nIt's bad. :( And tell your guys too — make sure they all say the same thing. Went to Agroprom and hasn't come back yet.
Incoming: \nConsider it done. Where can I find you if something comes up?
Outgoing: \nSorry, can't say just yet. I'll find you myself. Once things settle down...
Incoming: \nUnderstood... Take care.
Outgoing: \nIf everything works out, I'll make it up to you. I won't forget.
Incoming: \nDon't worry about it. We'll square up later.
Incoming: \nSo what did you dig up?
Outgoing: \nWhere?
Incoming: \nWhere, where... in the middle of nowhere. That underground lab you were talking about, remember?
Outgoing: \nAre you stupid? I was just passing along what people were saying. We weren't even planning to go... You try getting in there. And if you do get in, you won't get back out...
Incoming: \nThen what was the point of bringing it up? I heard some hotshot went down there. They say he hauled out so much stuff he could buy out the whole Zone.

Outgoing: \nHa! Keep listening to that... Someone's just talking himself up. What a joke... The way I heard it, nobody can even find the entrance, and you're telling me someone "hauled stuff out..." Just talk.
Incoming: \nAnd how do you know nobody can find the entrance? Someone must have been looking... and probably not just to look — they wanted to go down there.
Outgoing: \nWell then, go ahead and look, and go down there yourself if you're so brave.
Incoming: \nAlright, alright... But still — if you hear anything, you'll let me know?
Outgoing: \nOf course!
Incoming: \nHey.
Outgoing: \nYeah...
Incoming: \nHeard about Kartavy? What do you make of it?
Outgoing: \nWhat is there to say — he lost it... Walked straight into an anomaly himself.
Incoming: \nYou think it was himself?

Outgoing: \nAbsolutely. The guys were right there, watched him pacing around. Thought he was trying to find a way around it, but it was the opposite — he was lining himself up to make sure. Then he ran and jumped straight in.
Incoming: \nDamn... He just snapped?
Outgoing: \nHe was already gone before that. Screaming at night, and during the day going on and on about some piece of the Monolith. Became a total wreck...
Incoming: \nDo you remember, about a month ago, there was another guy going on about some Monolith fragments too?
Outgoing: \nBobos or whatever his name was, Gundos?

Incoming: \nYeah, him. So I saw him recently — didn't even recognize him. Same thing basically — screaming at night, raving about the Monolith during the day. And then they found him hanged a few days ago. Someone said he'd made enemies and the gopniks strung him up. But I think it was himself. He'd been like a ghost lately — who could he have pissed off that badly... He cracked too. There's something to all this...
Outgoing: \nCoincidence, probably.
Incoming: \nSo what is this piece of the Monolith anyway? Some kind of artifact?
Outgoing: \nOh, come on... I don't know and I don't want to know. I've got enough trouble with Jellyfish as it is.
Incoming: \nBut still?
Outgoing: \nIt's just nonsense. Why would you listen to every lunatic out there?
Incoming: \nWell, there must be something to it... Forget it. I'll look into it myself and let you know.
Outgoing \nTo hell with it! Don't you dare! I don't even want to know — I sleep better that way.
Outgoing: \nWhen will you have the money? I'm waiting.
Incoming: \nHey! We agreed — the thirtieth!
Outgoing: \nI need it now.
Incoming: \nWhere am I supposed to get it now? I don't have a job until the day after tomorrow.
Outgoing: \nFigure it out, but I need the money by the day after tomorrow. I bailed you out, didn't I? Now it's your turn to bail me out.
Incoming: \nI don't have it right now! And there's nothing to sell, you know that!
Outgoing: \nYou have a rifle, don't you? Go see Sidor — he put out a job, wants someone taken care of, pays well.
Incoming: \nNo, I'm not a hired gun. I'm not doing that. Why don't you go yourself?

Outgoing: \nI'm busy with something else right now! I've got to hand over a pile of cash to the crew the day after tomorrow. I'm heading out on a job too, I'll be back tomorrow morning. I'll have part of the money, and I need your share on top of that. I don't care, you understand? Find it wherever you can, otherwise I'm handing your debt over to the crew.
Incoming: \nHave you completely lost your mind?
Outgoing: \nThat's all. Consider yourself warned.
Incoming: \nAre you at Agro? What the hell is going on at Agroprom?!
Outgoing: \nLots of things going on over there. Which one in particular? :)

Incoming: \nThis: the military reinforced the checkpoint on the route from the Garbage. Round-the-clock watch, day and night. And nobody I ask knows anything about it. Do you know anything? Maybe someone on your end has heard something?
Outgoing: \nDoesn't seem like it. Can you negotiate with them? No way?
Incoming: \nThey won't even let you near — almost shot me, fired warning shots over my head.

Outgoing: \nWell... I just asked Sidor — he's playing dumb. Says he'll look into it. Something's definitely off. Maybe they're running an operation? Try again tomorrow — they probably won't hold the reinforcement for long. Guaranteed it's a one-time thing.
Incoming: \nWhat do you mean, tomorrow?! Sanya Stepnoy fell into some pit over there, broke his leg, can't get out. He'll bleed out! Ask Sidor to at least let me through alone!

Outgoing: \nSidor says — not before the day after tomorrow. Turns out the army brass has been poking around there for two days already, you follow? Don't even try — they've got elite special forces guarding it.
Incoming: \nBastards. Sanya is going to die, looks like. What a stupid way for things to go...
Outgoing: \nWhat can I do? That's the Zone, pal...
Incoming: \nHey. Have you been to the swamp in the Valley lately?
Outgoing: \nWell... what do you want?

Incoming: \nIn the Dark Valley, all the grass around the swamps turned yellow overnight. In the morning we headed out to the swamps for artifacts, made it to the bridge, then turned back. We don't know if it's safe to go there. Everything around is green, but near the swamps — yellow. Unnatural. Like someone painted it.
Outgoing: \nAre there any Flesh wandering around there?
Incoming: \nNothing. Like everything just died out. That's the scary part. But a whole crop of artifacts showed up there overnight. There are even capsules just lying around, can you believe it?
Outgoing: \nWrite to Semyonov. He knows about this kind of thing.
Incoming: \nAlready wrote to him. He's not answering. And here we are — want to go, scared to go, and common sense says don't.
Outgoing: \nTo hell with it, don't go. You only live once, as they say.
Incoming: \nYeah. Still hurts though...
Incoming: \nSo what's going on, where are you? Haven't forgotten about our talk?
Outgoing: \nWhich one? Remind me. I was a bit out of it yesterday, might've forgotten...
Incoming: \nDid you get drunk yesterday? We were supposed to go on a run. That's no way to do things.
Outgoing: \nYeah, a little. I remember the run, just remind me where and why...

Incoming: \nGet lost! I'm not going anywhere with you — you've probably already started nursing your hangover, and I'm supposed to drag you along? No thanks. I marched out to the farm like an idiot, thought you'd already be there... You left me hanging.
Outgoing: \nWhy are you so sore about it? Look, I'll make it up to you — I've got some ammo in a stash... Don't worry. I really can't go today anymore. We'll link up another time.
Incoming: \nForget it, you're all talk. Talking's the only thing you're good at...
Outgoing: \nHeard a chopper went down?
Incoming: \nHeard it, so what?
Outgoing: \nThey say it was hauling supplies for the army. Provisions, all kinds of stuff. We should stock up for free. What do you say?
Incoming: \nHow do you know it was supplies, and for the army?

Outgoing: \nVovan brought back a whole crate of canned stew, could barely carry it. Going around all proud of himself now. The chopper's got military guards on it. The crate just got thrown far from the crash, and he grabbed it.
Incoming: \nMaybe it wasn't from the chopper — maybe it was just sitting there...
Outgoing: \nSure... what just sits around in the Zone? Definitely from the chopper. And if there's one crate, there's more.
Incoming: \nLike the military are just going to let you walk up. They're really looking forward to seeing you.
Outgoing: \nWe work something out, make a trade. Worth a shot... Maybe there's still some unclaimed stuff lying around somewhere.
Incoming: \nNo, I'm not going. Try explaining to them we're there on legitimate business... They'll shoot us for looting...
Outgoing: \nSuit yourself. Don't come asking later.
Outgoing: \nHey, buddy. I'm in trouble. :(
Incoming: \nWhat is it now?
Outgoing: \nI went to that place, had a look. But can't get to it alone, and there are Snorks crawling all over. Barely fought my way out. One of them bit me. :(
Incoming: \nJust disinfect the bite with iodine or antiseptic and wrap it up.
Outgoing: \nThat's what I did. I'm just worried something might happen. I won't turn into a Snork, will I?

Incoming: \nYou probably just snagged yourself on a branch from the shock when you saw the Snork. :) All that stuff — vampires, werewolves and the rest — it's just stories. What, do you think you're going to grow a trunk now? A dog bit me once and I didn't turn into a dog. Forget about it! You're alive, that's what matters. Get yourself patched up, then we'll go together.
Outgoing: \nA dog is one thing, a Snork is another. They say their saliva is venomous. I won't grow a trunk, sure, but what if I start degenerating and end up like them...
Incoming: \nStop whining!

Outgoing: \nWell, Maloy told me how a Bloodsucker got him once. He says now his blood doesn't clot properly. Gets worse every day. He's already scared to cut sausage — what if he nicks himself and can't stop the bleeding.

Incoming: \nMaloy made that Bloodsucker story up himself. If one had actually "got him," the dogs would've finished what was left of Maloy long ago. He's always been a liar and a coward, and always will be.
Outgoing: \nEasy for you to say — you haven't been bitten by a Snork...
Incoming: \nTell the guys that if you start growling, they should shoot you before you bite anyone else. :)
Outgoing: \nAll you can do is joke. And my whole arm has swollen up. :(
Incoming: \nGood grief! Enough! I'm switching off — I'm sick of your whining.
Incoming: \nHow are things over there? What are you up to?
Outgoing: \nNothing. Sitting around, waiting.
Incoming: \nWaiting for what? For the weather to clear up?
Outgoing: \nNo... waiting for some truck.
Incoming: \nWhat?!

Outgoing: \nWell, we worked something out with the eggheads. They threw us a job: according to their calculations, some truck is supposed to pass through here. We need to spot it, photograph everything. For the trouble they gave us seven days' worth of food. How they figured all that out, I have no idea — they just said if the truck doesn't show up this week, then there's no point waiting any longer.
Incoming: \nAre you out of your minds?! They're obviously trying to catch the "truck of death"!

Outgoing: \nWhat do we care... It's a freebie. Our job is to wait and take pictures — it's not like we're going to go chasing corpses... And there's even payment on top of that if it works out. Think about it — nothing to do except sit in one spot for a week and that's it. And we don't even have to go looking for food.
Incoming: \nAnd what are you going to photograph — the dust from the wheels?

Outgoing: \nNo... the road dead-ends into a pile of rubble up ahead. It'll either crash, or stop, or turn around. If it shows up at all. It's fine... We can't eat through all this food anyway, we're not doing anything and we're not even drinking vodka, so there'll be plenty left over even if we don't get any shots. And if we do get shots, they'll pay us on top. Either way, we come out ahead.
Incoming: \nAnd how do they know a truck will come through there?
Outgoing: \nAsk them yourself. Maybe because they've spotted these trucks everywhere else but not here yet? I don't know — that's what eggheads are for, to figure things out.
Incoming: \nI wouldn't have agreed to it... have you heard what people say about those trucks?

Outgoing: \nYou didn't want to go to the Valley either, always shaking in your boots — "it's dangerous here, it's dangerous there"... that's exactly why you're always broke. Smart people find easy work and don't go trembling like you do...
Incoming: \nI'm not trembling. But I still wouldn't wait around for any truck.
Outgoing: \nRelax — all those stories about them are just tall tales... Alright, I'll fill you in later.
Incoming: \nSure.
Incoming: \nHey, buddy. I'm in a bad way. :(
Outgoing: \nSame as before — bad. :( Waiting on some doctor. The guys passed word that he'll come.
Incoming: \nA real doctor?
Outgoing: \nHow should I know. I'm not exactly in a position to be picky right now.
Incoming: \nRight... At least is the place you're holed up reliable?
Outgoing: \nIt's alright. The guys left some food and water, I'll last a couple of days... probably... at least there's signal here. They said they'd come back by.
Incoming: \nWhy didn't they just carry you out right away?
Outgoing: \nThey didn't dare... Say they're afraid I'll fall apart if they move me... heh.
Incoming: \nStill cracking jokes? Good man... Hang in there.
Outgoing: \nWhat else can I do... Truth be told, I've been thinking back over my life...
Incoming: \nYeah?
Outgoing: \nIt's all been pretty bad... and there's no fixing any of it, damn it all... :(
Incoming: \nHey, cut that out! Everything's going to be fine — the doctor will come and we'll get you out.
Outgoing: \nCome on, who are you trying to fool... Listen, you... write to you know who, you know where...
Incoming: \nEnough of that! Just hang on! At least for these couple of days, I'm asking you... He'll definitely come. I'll find him myself and drag him to you by the collar!
Outgoing: \nI'll try...
Incoming: \nHey :)
Outgoing: \nWho's this? What do you want?
Incoming: \nIt's Kolyan. You remember Agrik?
Outgoing: \nOh! Hey, pal, sorry, didn't recognize you. You had a different handle before, I think... Thanks again for the meds, you really came through.
Incoming: \nDon't mention it. :) Hey, do you know Lyokha Kot?
Outgoing: \nUmm... the one who was bragging that he took down a Boar with a bayonet knife on a dare? Sat around the campfire with him a couple of times — why?

Incoming: \nYeah, him. I'm looking for him. If you see him, pass it on — Kolyan Denezhka dropped his PDA against the wall when he was drunk, got a new one, and well... he's rebuilding his contacts. Tell him not to message me for now if he's looking for me... And my handle is definitely different now, decided to change it with the new PDA. Got tired of the old one...
Outgoing: \nI'll pass it on. So... how are you holding up?
Incoming: \nCan't get through to him. Thanks, take care.
Incoming: \nSo have you decided? Are you coming with us or what?
Outgoing: \nHow far is the hike exactly? And what's the situation like out there in general?
Incoming: \nWell, who's going to give you a straight answer on that, friend? It'll be whatever it turns out to be. But like Mokryak said: a week of hell and you'll be rolling in it. So — are you in?
Outgoing: \nIf everyone else is going, then so am I, obviously! When do we move out?
Incoming: \nTomorrow we stock up. You do the same — nobody's going to carry your sausage and ammo for you. And be there sharp the day after tomorrow, don't be late!
Outgoing: \nI'll be there.
Outgoing: \nTyulen, honest to God, you're a fool. What have you done?
Incoming: \nWhat are you on about?

Outgoing: \nYesterday you were singing away by the campfire about how the Forgotten Village has so much loot you couldn't haul it out with a dump truck! Do you have any idea how many people are going to rush out there now? It's probably a madhouse already. Even the dogs and the Cats are bolting out of there with their eyes popping out of their heads — never seen so many stalkers in one place! What were you thinking, blabbing like that?.. There won't be anything left to find there now but trampled grass!
Incoming: \nWatch what you say to me...
Outgoing: \nThe point is, Tyulen, that's not how things are done. What's ours is ours — you don't go shooting your mouth off about it...

Incoming: \nYou're going to tell me what to do? Do I owe you something? About yesterday... were you cutting a path with me through the Forgotten Village? Or maybe you were watching my back while I was finding the way?
Outgoing: \nDid you ever share anything with me? Good people vouched for you and asked me to keep an eye on you, so I did. Now I can see exactly who — or rather what — you are...
Incoming: \nFine... then I don't know you either anymore.
Incoming: \nDo you know where I can get my suit repaired?
Outgoing: \nWhat do you mean where? Head to the Bar — didn't you know?
Incoming: \nI know. Only that's out of the question. Those red-and-blacks have declared me persona non grata there. Got a message from a Duty member today. They figured it out...
Outgoing: \nWell, damn... What happened? How did it come to that?

Incoming: \nI stumbled into an anomaly, barely dragged myself out. Suit was completely wrecked, nothing left but rags, I was half out of my mind — fell down right next to it and heard the bushes rustling, so I let both barrels loose into them.
Outgoing: \nDead?

Incoming: \nI don't know. They hit the ground straight away and lobbed a couple of grenades. The moment I realized I'd shot at Duty members I was already on all fours, and that's how I took off. The anomaly saved me — threw the shrapnel upward — and I just kept crawling on all fours all the way to the Garbage. Now I'm lying here, messed up. Turns out I broke my left arm, can you believe it.
Outgoing: \nOh, damn... Alright, hold on, let me just...

Outgoing: \nOkay, here's the deal. I talked to a guy. Goes by Prokhor. He said one of them took a round through the arm — Lepyokha Sery. But he says he saw him — nothing serious hit him... He's on his feet and walking patrol already, just around the base for now. They're reasonable guys overall — I told them what happened, we talked it through. Bottom line — once you're back on your feet a bit, come on over. I've got three liters, and you'll owe each of them one artifact each — two total, so get them ready. They're not asking for more than that.
Incoming: \nPal, thank you! I owe you, honest to God!
Outgoing: \nAh, don't worry about it — we'll square up. :))

Outgoing: \nYou know what I saw yesterday? Some new anomaly. White sparks flying up and down. I threw a pebble into it, and it just hung there for a couple of minutes, then started spinning, and whoosh! — gone.

Incoming: \nOne of these days a pebble's going to come flying back and crack you on the head, and that'll be the end of your experiments. Go to the scientists, ask them to take you on as a lab assistant — you can push science forward and be a blessing to humanity. :))

Outgoing: \nWash their test tubes? No thanks, I'll manage on my own — besides, I need to look out for myself first. I'm just curious — it's such a strange thing, appearing out of nowhere with no explanation. Remember when I tried to photograph that emission?
Incoming: \nOh... when you set your point-and-shoot on a timer, right? Yeah, I remember. Though you said the camera was toast after that.
Outgoing: \nThat's what I thought — left it in a stash, but then I started going through my stuff, picked it up and switched it on...
Incoming: \nAnd?
Outgoing: \nThe first frames are pure black, then overexposed — just solid white blur, but the last two... there's a face.
Incoming: \nWhose?
Outgoing: \nLooks like a woman's. Hard to tell — I had the camera aimed at the sky, so it's not clear. Anyway, when we meet up I'll show you.

Log: \nWhat in the hell did I come here for? A ro-o-omantic, for crying out loud... Even back at the station, some shady type ripped me off, promising to get me through "no problem"... and he did. No problem, sure. Ha — takes a fool to make the world go round! And the hunger is something awful. There is ab-so-lute-ly nothing here. This Cordon we're stuck on, dazed by everything going on around us, is mind-numbing. At least the one in charge here — goes by Wolf — keeps some semblance of order. All sorts turn up. Yesterday some weasel was going through the bags, trying to steal something — the guys caught him and shot him behind the fence. No room for humanism here, that's for sure... And I'm scared. Very scared.

Log: \nI watched some poor kid get his leg crushed in an anomaly — idiot went diving for an artifact without thinking... The way he screamed, God it was awful. What were we supposed to do with him? We managed to pull him back, dragged him out, bandaged him up — I gave a couple of my own bandages — but what then? We tied a tourniquet above the knee and carried him inside. Went around avoiding each other's eyes after that. The poor wretch screamed for two days! Who's going to look after him now when we're sitting here like idiots ourselves. What the hell made me come here... Why? Go ahead and try to grab those artifacts when you have no idea what they'll do to you — irradiate you to death, or turn your mind inside out...

Log: \nAnd the mutants? Nothing touches these filthy things! The other day three of us couldn't put down a single dog. And what kind of dog is it, really — it's something else entirely. They say it's blind, but it moves like a rocket, rotten all over, hideous. The smell is unbearable. Though I'm no better — nowhere to wash up around here. To hell with all of it! I need to get home...

Log: \nFunny... It feels like chasing the tail of a moving train... hell, I don't even know what to compare it to! ...Couple of months ago I stopped by a real estate agency, and they say prices are going up — ha! Scraped together every last ruble I'd earned and saved, showed up feeling like a big shot, and it turned out I could only afford a one-room Khrushchevka on the outskirts... Tanya, I suppose, doesn't much care where I've gone off to "earn money" this time. Up north? A shift job? Might as well be outer space. As long as I bring back cash — that's all that matters.

Log: \nBought her a car recently — now she walks from the store to the car, and from the car to the apartment with her nose in the air, not even looking at the neighbors... funny. What do I know — maybe she's already seeing that mechanic at the auto shop. Not like I ever went there... And my Lyubashka will probably get married without ever getting an apartment. I just can't seem to get her a wedding gift in time. :) That's IT! I can't take it anymore. I'm exhausted. And Tanya is cheating — no question. Though you can understand it, but then again on the other hand...

Log: \nFunny — the story about catching a chill after the emergency shift satisfied her. She wrinkled her nose and asked whether I was planning to go to a hospital. Sure I am. Specialists in radiation sickness are just crawling out of the walls around here. Oh, what a life! All my hopes are on the artifacts... And I'll knock Tanya's teeth out, the tramp! If I find out she's with that mechanic, I'll beat her! But that's a lie. I'm lying to myself. Truth is I couldn't care less who she's with or how. I must be sick after all. Those three minutes under the emission won't pass without consequences — everyone told me so. These nightmares...

Log: \nProbably my subconscious trying to shake me up. And honestly — I've started coming to the Zone like it's a regular job. Got used to it, treat it like routine. Mutants, anomalies — none of it bothers me anymore. The only thing that matters is the loot and the cash for the loot! And yet somewhere deep down there's a certainty burning that this isn't what really matters. I used to be scared of the Zone something fierce, and now I can't do without it. It doesn't feel like adrenaline addiction — it's something else entirely...

Log: \nAnd these nightmares — they're something else! Can't sleep without vodka anymore. Such a whirl of chaos in my head at night, it's enough to make you cry. The guys threw a boot at me once because I was screaming like a stuck pig. Nobody has it easy out here, sure, but apparently I was really going at it, they said...

Log: \nWhere does all this come from? Is it just me having these dreams or everyone? Everything feels just as real in the dream as it does when I'm awake. Except I do terrible things in them. Recently I dreamed that I was looking for Ivan Sky on the Cordon for some reason, and he was asking me to bring him something. I had no intention of bringing anyone anything — instead I tossed a grenade into the campfire while the guys were sitting around it. I remember Ivan's glassy, dead herring eyes, I remember going through his corpse, slipping in the blood next to the fire... Madness? Madness! What is this garbage...

Log: \n...Stopped by the Cordon. Everything's the same there. Ivan was getting ready to head to the Swamps, asked me to come along... I stalled for a long time, then told him about my nightmare. He got very uneasy for some reason and started rushing off somewhere right away... I got the feeling that it didn't really matter where he was going, as long as it was away from me.

Log: \n...Vodka's garbage. You drink and don't get drunk... Yesterday there was a message on the local net about the death of some former Clear Sky member. Managed to track down people who were there — they're talking about a grenade in a campfire. Three dead, two wounded. A chill came over me all of a sudden...

Log: \n...Couldn't fight it off and fell asleep. In the dream I was wandering through some underground passages, killing people in leather jackets and track pants — all dressed the same, all with the same face, strongly reminding me of someone. I clearly remember taking a bullet through the head. Compared to the body — it doesn't really hurt, just a dull impact, and that's it... for some reason the dream always starts with a burned-out campfire or some kind of fuse...

Log: \nDamn these dreams! I'm starting to wonder if I'm slowly losing my mind... It all feels so real... and somehow tied to reality. And which is more real, I can no longer tell... I'm definitely losing my mind. I can't take it anymore. Killed twenty people in military uniforms in some three-story building. A loudspeaker blaring... you can hear a helicopter circling somewhere... And I'm slowly sinking, sinking, sinking... straight down into an underground passage. And I see a semi-transparent figure... And blood everywhere. Everything soaked in blood...
Log: \nI've been lying here for the fourth day now (or fifth, I don't remember anymore), no strength to get up. I think this is it, the end...

Log: \nWe're sitting in an old but still mostly intact cellar, hiding from the emission. God, the smell! We stink worse than bums... These suits don't let your body breathe at all, and on top of that we're always soaking wet from fear. Hygiene is completely out of the question here. Old mattresses thrown right onto the rotted floorboards are crawling with insects. Woodlice and centipedes don't even bother me anymore, but the lice... Come on, let the emission end already, come on!

Log: \n...Lay half the day in some putrid puddle hiding from a patrol. A military group was clearing the area for the scientists. Didn't manage to slip away in time. Even my breathing was cautious — I was half-frozen to death. Drank the last half-bottle like water. Shivering.

Log: \n...Found an artifact near an anomaly. A pale yellow clump of something with red drops on it. It was floating about ten centimeters off the ground, bobbing and spinning. I poked it with a stick — the artifact jumped like a balloon and started rolling and drifting toward the anomaly. Grabbed it with my hand like an idiot, and it hit me like an electric shock or a sack of bricks. Came to with blood on my face and blood running from my nose, but thank God the artifact hadn't gone anywhere — it was still bouncing around nearby. Rolled it with a stick into a plastic bag that had bread in it.

Log: \n...Damn, they won't let up... On my way back, dreaming of haggling with a fence, some grim type stepped out from behind a collapsed shed with a hunting rifle and demanded I hand over the loot. In conditions like these the veneer of civilization wears off a person very quickly — I'd already had the pleasure of learning that — so I'd had the sawn-off at the ready long before I reached the village. Apparently this one didn't even have time to understand what was happening. Or couldn't bring himself to pull the trigger? That's also something you have to learn...

Log: \n...The type's friends cornered me in a ravine. They're not actually that dangerous, honestly — but the local wildlife is something else... And it looks like they've already caught my scent. Need to get out of here before it's too late...
Outgoing: \nWant to hear something funny?
Incoming: \nWhat? Go on...
Outgoing: \nOver on the Cordon, Sidorovich ordered a "Stone Blood" from some fresh-faced rookie. The kid asks him: "Wait, can a rock even bleed?"...
Incoming: \nWhere's the funny part?

Outgoing: \nHold on... Sidrych goes: "This is the Zone, son. Anything is possible here." And this blockhead walked past the road and started blasting away at rocks with everything he had, trying to draw blood. :)) Burned through a ton of ammo, never did get any blood out of a rock. Lucky a military patrol spotted him in time and he bolted off somewhere toward the ATP.
Incoming: \n:) What a character... Some people are just born idiots...
Incoming: \nHey, are you busy?
Outgoing: \nNo, sitting by a little fire right now, managed to get some tea. Oh, it's good — haven't had any in ages. All vodka, vodka, getting sick of it already. :) What did you need?
Incoming: \nI'm not even sure how to put this... How are you holding up after the last emission?
Outgoing: \nSeems alright... what about you? Did you catch something?

Incoming: \nSeems like no, but I'm spooked all the time. Something's off. Nightmares, my head running through all kinds of stuff. Here's one example: our generator at the base died. By the time anyone sorted it out, evening had hit. But the light bulbs are still glowing! Didn't notice during the day, but once it got dark — there you go — glowing. The shadows from these bulbs are kind of blue and they seem to be moving on their own, I'm telling you! I'm standing there looking at my own shadow and it's waving at me... I'm even scared to go take a leak alone now, I keep hanging around the guys the whole time and they're giving me looks... What the hell is going on?
Outgoing: \nNo anti-rads, I take it? Just treating everything with vodka? Well, that's what happens when you keep treating...
Incoming: \nWhat do you mean?
Outgoing: \nI mean drink less! Switch to tea, like we did here. That's your whole problem right there.
Incoming: \nThe DTs, you think? Oh man! And here I thought... Got myself scared for nothing. Well, thanks — that puts my mind at ease...

Incoming: \nThe other day in the Gut I stumbled across a downed military stalker patrol group. Someone had already picked through them good before I got there, and the dogs had already had their fill. Then something possessed me to climb down into a little ravine nearby. Found a massive iron case down there, plastered all over with seals. Couldn't crack it open on the spot. So get this — I lugged it all the way to my stash on the Cordon. Cursed the whole way there: the damn thing must weigh 25 kilos! Now I'm sitting here thinking: what for? What am I supposed to do with this case?
Outgoing: \nGive it to Sidorovich.
Incoming: \nHow did I not figure that out myself! He pays really well for odds and ends like that. Thanks, Captain Obvious! :)
Incoming: \nHow much longer do we have to sit around waiting for you? We'll leave without you.

Outgoing: \nDamned if I know. Might not be worth waiting. Kuznetsov hasn't been at his post under the bridge for three days! And his replacement got killed. So the legit way across the embankment is off the table! Won two rounds against Stas and cleaned out his whole vodka stash (was going to swap it for ammo from Kuznetsov). Looks like I'll have to drink it instead.
Incoming: \nWell then, don't let a drop go to waste! Take care.
Outgoing: \nGood luck to you. Either way I've got next to no ammo. What kind of run can you do with a loadout like that. Another time.

Outgoing: \nOn the subject of artifact cooking. Some of them flash when they pop out, some don't. Pick them up and try again. Usually on the second or third pass after placing the second ingredient (per the recipe) — for SF that's a bandage — the anomaly gives a long accepting flash and pulls in the rest of the ingredients on its own.
Incoming: \nSF? \nFlash?
Outgoing: \nSF — Stone Flower. \nFlash (Ukrainian slang) — means to flicker or sparkle (referring to light). (Google comes through again)
Incoming: \nAh, got it. And how do you pick the right anomaly? They say you can't cook in just any one.

Outgoing: 
When picking an anomaly (any type) for cooking, make sure: 
1. The ingredients don't scatter; 
2. You can easily pick up the result — meaning if you're cooking in a "Whirligig", stand a little lower, or the cooked artifact might fly off into the stratosphere.
Outgoing: \nI just cannot get past the northern checkpoint! The moment I try, they're already right there. I have to turn tail every time.
Incoming: \nWho's "they"? Bandit thugs — looters?
Outgoing: \nIf only! At least you can negotiate with those. Tushkanos...
Incoming: \nTushkanos? Come on, man... Have you tried shooting them?
Outgoing: \nTried that. They're just too damn hard to kill, and I'm still green — can't hit anything.

Incoming: \nAlright, listen here, "greenie" — they've got a nest there. That's what they're protecting. So grit your teeth and run like hell until you're past the nest. The Tushkanos will back off on their own. Sure, they'll bite you on the backside a couple of times. But that's nothing. You can show off the scars to the girls later — something to brag about.
Outgoing: \nThanks for the tip, old man. Going to try your recommendation right now.
Incoming: \nGo for it. And once you're through — drop me a line. There's already bets riding on you out here...

Log: \nHeard plenty of times that you can booby-trap a stash, but what's the right way to do it? Just put grenades in it? Because those are exactly what got stolen from me... \nMaybe I had no business coming to the Zone... This isn't for me...
Outgoing: \nWhat the hell? Lyoha Tambovski is wandering around Agro. I come out to the Garbage — and who do you think it is? Lyoha Tambovski, but a different mug and different gear. More Zone tricks?
Incoming: \nNope. Just two Lyokhas. Both from Tambov.

Outgoing: \nAra, listen — with great hardship I get forty bottle. From Bar I carry 20, another 20 Stas he guard at Sidor. I come Cordon — no Sidor... business trip. One sleep — no Sidor. Two sleep — no Sidor. Three sleep — again no! On fourth morning he come back, tourist! Here you go, take loot, give vodka! Run to embankment — Kovalchuk... One sleep — Kovalchuk, two sleep — Kovalchuk. On third — hurray! Kuzya return. Here, Kuzya, take your vodka, give AK-47. Vakh, good! Only five day total I sleep.

Outgoing: \nI've got a stalemate situation on my hands... Grabbed a load of vodka from Stas — and headed to Kuznetsov to trade. But he's not there. Some young guy standing in his place instead. Went and slept for three days. Same one again. Slept another three days — same Kovalchuk... What am I supposed to do? Who do I offload the vodka to?
Incoming: \nWhile you've been sleeping, that's when Kuzya's been on duty.

Log: \nSidorovich left. For two days. Forced to drag Prapor's crate around with me everywhere. Heavy as sin, the damn thing! Rations are running low. Bought out everything the stalkers in the village were willing to part with. Had to make a run to the Garbage and strong-arm the bandit thugs for some. Still not enough. And someone cleaned out the stash on the roof. Left only matches and a couple of girlie magazines. Meanwhile everyone else is sitting around the campfire with vodka. They all complain: "Feeling kind of down, let's have a drink." Could've offered me some, at least... Two days gone — still no Sidor. And will he even come back?

Letter: Part 1 \nWith greetings and wishes for long years and nothing but good fortune — your friend for life! I already wrote to you before about how my stash on the Garbage got "warmed up" and someone made off with a pile of good stuff, including an AK cooked in a Fruit Punch, a stalker jacket upgraded by Docent, a "Spring", a Saiga, and various vodkas, medkits, and sausages. I asked the old hands — is it worth getting upset about and launching an inquiry? The "Good Samaritans" hinted — yes, it is. \nSo I decided to start the investigation at the Hangar on the Garbage. I go around greeting my colleagues, making small talk, while keeping an eye out — who's got what piece in their hands.

Letter: Part 2 \nAnd I come across a certain Barabashka. The mug, to put it plainly... Not that I'm Stirlitz myself, but this one either had French ancestors or just bears the mark of genetic degradation all on his own. And what's telling — he's swinging a Saiga around with a very casual air. But what really put me on edge was when he offered to sell me a jacket, exactly like mine. Sure, maybe it's just the same model — but they're not exactly lying around on every corner...

Letter: Part 3 \nWell then, alrighty, I think to myself. Let me have a poke around the nearby stashes. Naturally, in the very first one I find (three cheers!!!) my cooked rifle and the rest of my rightful property, including the "Spring". And a tripwire. Naturally, a message comes in about getting my face rearranged. Signed by Monsieur Barabashka. Which is precisely what was needed. Incidentally, along with the threats came a helpful suggestion that it would be better not to run into the stash's owner anymore. Oh sure, RIGHT! As they say in the cartoons: "You're just the one I needed! You, my good sir, have seen fit to eat MY carrot..."

Letter: Part 4 \nI picked up my little Kalash (already repaired and loaded, thank you very much, Barabashka!) and headed off to the showdown with the "degenerate". And there he is already waiting for me, giving a little salute from his (my) Saiga right at my head. Didn't kill me — just winged me a bit. His mistake. I squeezed off a short burst — and no more Barabashka. Everyone around was satisfied, nobody cried over him — he started it, not me. That's how I restored justice with my own two hands...
Outgoing: \nCan anyone tell me how the scientists at Yantar behave when it comes to theft? Thinking of making a stash right inside the bunker. Hoping science hasn't gotten that desperate in the Zone.
Incoming: \nSeems like they don't steal. I personally kept weapons, armor and other junk there. All fine.

Incoming: \nDuty members steal and re-hide stuff somewhere nearby — like children. Like mean children — the bastards booby-trap it! So then you find it but can't just take it. :( The scientists there are alright though, they don't even suspect anyone's stashing things with them. Unless it's vodka, mind you.
Outgoing: \nDo I really have to go back through the same tunnel with the Electros? But how?

Incoming: \nListen here, son. Get out a bolt and wait for the last Electro in front of you to go dark. Run forward and stop just before the active Electro, leaving enough space for the next one to appear. Watch for when the Electro behind the active one goes dark. The moment it does, throw the bolt at the active Electro to discharge it, then bolt through. Same thing with the second set of Electros — and you're at Sidor's.
Outgoing: \nWell, I'll be damned... No way I'm getting through that.
Incoming: \nScared? And not even embarrassed to broadcast it...
Outgoing: \nYep, I want to live, not embarrassed to say it, and don't plan on starting to be.
Incoming: \nWell then, off you go... Humiliate yourself for some canned stew...
Outgoing: \nOff I go then. And not for canned stew... For sausage...
Outgoing: \nArkasha, have you ever tried a flamethrower? Is it worth renting one?
Incoming: \nNever tried a flamethrower — too heavy, and in my stalker suit every gram counts.
Outgoing: \nGot it. So there's no point in me bothering with one either.
Incoming: \nPakhom, you so... son of a dog! I hauled him a jar of "Fruit Punch", dumped it on the counter, and he just grunted and won't even talk to me.
Outgoing: \nPakhom doesn't like repeating himself. And he doesn't like it when people don't listen properly. He said A COUPLE OF JARS, and you brought one.
Incoming: \nI thought it was just a figure of speech... Pakhom is a man of principle, I'll give him that!

Log: \nOn repairs: \nDon't forget to remove everything that isn't factory — ammo, scopes, etc. — they'll give it back loaded, but any attachments you forget will go to charity. And good luck trying to prove otherwise afterward — you'll wear yourself out and get nowhere.

Log: \nAt the Swamps, at the old Clear Sky base, the trader who collects "Fruit Punch" — Pakhom, I think — has started buying animal limbs for very good money. Need to quietly pass this on to my guys.

Log: \nSpent a long time dealing with the army boys myself: pay up, hand over vodka, and if you try to sneak through they'll shoot you on the spot. So now I do it like this: I grab one of the crates at the dairy farm and drag it up to the wire (where the open container sits between the tunnel and the bridge). Getting in — two jumps over the wire, free of charge. Getting back — costs food: the invisible "Witch's Jelly" burns for far too long and far too unpleasantly.
Outgoing: \nWhere can I get more underbarrel grenades — I'm barely finding any. Also, is it worth picking up Sultan's shortened Dragunov? Good piece of kit? Anyone used one?
Incoming: \nFor which underbarrel launcher? If it's ours, do some work for Voronin — he'll sort you out. If it's a NATO one, you can trade a modificate to Sidor for some.

Outgoing: \nThe Duty guys at Yantar are something else! I'm clearing my head by the campfire near the bunker, and then an Electrochimera comes straight at the gate! I didn't even have time to get scared — they tore it apart with a couple of bursts just like that.
Incoming: \nThat's them alright! If they'd just stop going through other people's stashes — they'd be worth their weight in gold!
Outgoing: \nWell, lucky us! Second day in the Zone and we've already got a recipe for cooking a "Goldfish"! Tier three! We'll cook it, hand it to Sidor, and we can get out of here!
Incoming: \nAnd where did you dig that up?
Outgoing: \nWent behind the sheds to take a leak and picked up a scrap of paper on the way. Sitting here turning it over in my hands. Paper's gone soggy but you can still make it out.
Incoming: \nWell? Don't keep me in suspense.
Outgoing: \nNeeds a pseudodog tail, some Stone Blood and some "gr"..
Incoming: \nMaybe "grams"?

Outgoing: \nMaybe. There's also the word "flash"... hmm. Looks like this: you throw the tail into a Fruit Punch and pour in however many grams of Stone Blood until there's a flash — it should spark, I think... and you cook it for ten minutes. Only it's not clear what the Fruit Punch is supposed to be made of. Seems like there are pigs around here... They've mutated, sure, but maybe it'll do — as long as it doesn't blow up.

Incoming: \nAlright, I've got a tail — cut it off a dead dog. Smells a bit, but it's mostly intact. I've got a pot too, I'll find something to make Fruit Punch from. The only thing I don't get is what this Stone Blood is...
Outgoing: \nI can't figure that out either. Better ask around the veterans.
Incoming: \nWe can. Just be careful! Don't let them figure out we've got a recipe.

Log: \nI divide Snorks into two types... roughly, for my own purposes — regular and Yantar. Yantar, because that's where I first ran into ones like that. They look different — different coloring, different look somehow... and they're vicious as hell. But dumb — both kinds. Though sometimes something comes over them. When they come in a group, they can actually use tactics — one straight at you, one from the side, just keep turning to keep up.

Log: \nCaught an interesting moment today. Walking to Yantar loaded up, decided to slip quietly past the lowlifes at the unfinished building. Already near the mercs' trailer when the bastards spotted me. Traded shots with them — dropped two, wounded one. Then an emission, damn it all! I ducked into the trailer, thinking the wounded one was done for. Waited it out — went to finish collecting the haul, and the lowlife is alive! Felt sorry for him — just a kid. While I was pulling a medkit out of my backpack, he — bam! — gone, that lowlife. Vanished into thin air.
Incoming: \nI would've splattered that guard's brains all over Sidorovich's bunker after the first time he tried that, and my hand wouldn't have trembled one bit.
Outgoing: \nAnd how many brains have you splattered so far? With your steady hand.

Log: \nTried to cook a "Porcupine" from an "Urchin" — got the recipe — been dancing around several Burners, nothing works, it just sits in the Burner and hisses. Maybe there are other conditions? Read a prayer, bang a drum... to whoever gave me the recipe...

Incoming: \nHey guys, people... Can anyone tell me where the kill spot is on the Boars in the Dark Valley? The damn things won't go down — I'm aiming strictly for the eyes and barely doing anything... 300 rounds for two Boars. It's not like I'm going to be throwing grenades at them...
Outgoing: \nWell, if it were easy, nobody would be paying you good money to do it.
Incoming: \nFair point. But still — how?

Outgoing: \nThat's a special breed, near the swamps — armored. There's just one family of them, the rest are normal — buckshot takes them down easy. You can tell them apart by their color — the ones you can't penetrate are lighter. You can just leave them alone over one eye, if you want.
Outgoing: \nAnyone know where to get a chimera kidney?
Incoming: \nOoh, he needs a kidney... In the Dark Valley, on the hill, there's about six Chimeras running around. Go ask them.
Outgoing: \nBy the way, how do you take down an Electrochimera?
Incoming: \nGet some distance and shoot, or use grenades. Either you drop the Chimera, or you get away fast enough.

Incoming: \nI took down my first Chimera like this: near the helicopter — you know the spot — there's a brick wall running along the building with a ledge next to it. I climbed up and shot from there, the Chimera couldn't get out of the tunnel, a UAZ was blocking the way.
Outgoing: \nI reckon the Chimera didn't die from your bullets — it died of heartbreak that it never got to know you better. :)
Log: \nA neutral at the farm is selling ammo cheap... took the risk, bought some... seems to fire, seems to kill... but there's still a nagging feeling something's off.

Log: \nAt the abandoned car lot I was in shock — after shooting at some Tushkanos, I hit a "Bungee". Stalkers shouting: "Get over here!" And I'm standing there gulping medkits, trying to figure out where to run so I don't die... What a rush!

Log: \nFor what it's worth, my shotgun only flew out of my hands once — a Boar knocked it loose in the Swamps — I grabbed it and still won the fight. But then neither Vergas nor I could repair it ourselves with repair kits. Only the four-eyes agreed to do it for next to nothing — I was actually surprised, thought he'd turn me down too...
Log: \nCherkasy. Rechnoy Proyezd 2, building 1, apt. 29. Galya (Oksana's friend)
Outgoing: \nWhat's the door code to get into the lab?
Incoming: \nHave you tried just opening the door? If I recall, there was no code there. Or knock — maybe someone will answer.
Incoming: \nGot offered either an AK-103 or an AK-101. What would you recommend?

Outgoing: \nHard to say... depends what you're going for. The 101 has better accuracy and reliability. But the real question is the round. The 101 takes 5.56 — pretty rare around here, sometimes you have to haul all the way to the Warehouses for it. Now the 103 takes 7.62 — that's where the real stopping power is. And the ammo is everywhere, which is exactly why I picked it up back in the day.
Incoming: \nGot it, something to think about...

Log: \n"Whirligig." There's a whistle to it, mesmerizing — like the wind before an emission. And above the spot it's almost like a swarm of midges. Don't stand around gawking — pull yourself together and get away from it fast before it sucks you in.

Log: \n"Vortex." Sounds like a Whirligig, but dust and leaves spiral around it along the ground. And the air movement is more noticeable. The main thing is not to mix them up — if there's no "midge swarm" above the hum, that's a Vortex, not a Whirligig. To check, I throw a pinch of struck matches — something heavier is risky — might come back at you.

Log: \n"Springboard." Rumbles like a distant train. And ripples spread out from the spot, like a stone dropped in water. Once it gets you there's no breaking free — same nasty business as "Witch's Jelly," meaning invisible "Fruit Punch" — slows you down and tears you apart from inside. I call it "Dead Stop" in my head. Best not to get close.

Log: \n"Witch's Jelly," or invisible "Fruit Punch." Nasty stuff. Step in it and it'll eat right through your suit, and then you start bleeding. Pure chemistry — that's the only word for it. One good thing: if you move carefully and don't rush, you can hear it clearly (bubbles like that soup in the commercial). And if you throw something at the spot, a stain spreads out — what Sakharov calls a "toxic-chemical" color.

Outgoing: \nBad rumors spreading through the Zone. The mutants are changing. Survivors say the creatures have started taking down stalkers from a distance. Chimeras howl, and when they do the stalker freezes up like Sidorovich spotting an artifact — can't run, can't jump. And then the two-headed thing leaps and tears him apart.
Incoming: \nTall tales...

Incoming: \nI wouldn't be so sure... Heard it from reliable people: a Bloodsucker — get this — doesn't even seem to be reaching the poor stalker, and yet the bastard is draining his blood! Not with those hellish tentacles of his either — it's like he's got an invisible knife in his claw! And it cuts, cuts away slowly, the scumbag, bleeding him out! Stalkers come out of encounters with one looking like mummies wrapped in bandages! One guy said: from now on he's not leaving the Bar without a bag full of medkits and bandages. That's how it is.

Outgoing: \nWolf, we picked up a couple of your ragamuffins at the Garbage near the little swamp — sitting under a tree huddled up against each other. And notably, between the two of them all they had was one artifact belt with no pouches.
Incoming: \nDefinitely mine?

Outgoing: \nAt first we thought they were fags, were going to leave them for the Zombies to sort out — but then they started howling like Tushkanos in the cold... Anyway, they say they were on a job for you, when Borov's lot grabbed them and roughed them up for good measure. All they left them was their flashlights — and one each under the eye.
Incoming: \nIf they came from the Valley through the Gut — definitely mine.
Outgoing: \nYours, yours. We'll walk them to the camp, after that you're on your own — we can't afford to waste time on them, we've got our own plans.
Incoming: \nThanks, guys. I'll send Petruha with the lads to pick up the poor wretches.
Outgoing: \nThanks doesn't pour into a glass or hang on your belt.
Incoming: \nMessage received. I'm at base — come by after the run, we'll share whatever the Zone provides.
Outgoing: \nLiving the high life, pal, if you're tossing artifact cases around. I picked up your contraption at the Garbage.
Incoming: \nHow is it mine?

Outgoing: \nYour initials are on the lid — you scratched them in front of me when we were sitting at the campfire. Don't you care? It's practically new. Scrape the dried Zombie brains off it and you could sell it to Sidor — he won't give much, but enough to drink your sorrows away.
Incoming: \nThrow it out! I nearly kicked the bucket because of that case.
Outgoing: \nI love a good story. Come on, spill it.

Incoming: \nWhat's there to tell... Last week I found two capsules at Yantar — while I was packing them into the case, Snorks came up on me. I dropped it and climbed up a pipe. Burned through five magazines before they backed off.
Outgoing: \nAnd then? Don't keep me hanging!

Incoming: \nStop rushing me! I grabbed the case and headed to the bunker with the nerds for the night. Woke up — head's spinning, feeling sick. Then I see bullet holes in the case — turns out while I was dealing with those things I'd put holes in more than just them. Long story short, I caught one hell of a radiation dose. And on top of that they kicked me out of the bunker — can't show my face there now...

Outgoing: \nTough break... You should've taken it to Lisaped — sure, he's a bit fried from electricity, but the kid's sharp and knows his weapons. They say he never even held a gun before, but after he came to following a fall from a utility pole, those gunsmithing skills just came to him on their own. He'd have sealed up the case for you, cheap too.

Incoming: \nSure, I'll just go to Lisaped! Filka Lapot told me how before his last run he asked Lisaped to replace the terminals on his artifact belt pouches. Next day Filka heads to the Sanatorium with that belt, and there are dogs circling around. Filka had a "Bubble" on him — the kind that keeps dogs away — so he tossed it into a pouch and sparked it.
Outgoing: \nWell? Did it work?

Incoming: \nHow do I put this — I don't know what Lisaped did with those terminals, but instead of the "Bubble," Filka activated his right testicle, so the dogs scattered from his screaming, not from any artifact. He doesn't wear the belt anymore. But ever since, the moment he sees a dog he hits the deck, grabs his crotch and starts begging for medkits.

Outgoing: \nSaw Mole with a flashlight I've never seen the likes of in the Zone — wonder where he dug up a floodlight like that. Must eat through batteries the way "Black Mark" eats through your health.
Incoming: \nMole is an old digger — they say he was searching for Ivan the Terrible's library through the Kremlin's underground passages in Moscow.
Outgoing: \nAnd? Did he find it?

Incoming: \nSeems like he found something — he got into some kind of scuffle underground, either with rivals, or with the feds, or with the locals down there — they don't like having their peace disturbed. Anyway, he had to make himself scarce for a long time. Now he's crawling around under the Zone — put a team together, they're looking for something too.
Outgoing: \nAnd he brought that floodlight with him?
Incoming: \nPetrenko hooked him up with the flashlight. That schemer could bring you an atomic submarine or Angelina Jolie — as long as you've got the cash.
Outgoing: \nWell, good to know — I'll keep that in mind. And when did you last see Mole?

Incoming: \nA while back, but I've heard from people that after a run-in with the army boys in the Agroprom tunnel he's been licking his wounds. Word is he's planning to head to the Thicket, into the caves. Can't sit still.

Outgoing: \nWhat a life — some can't find happiness above ground, others find it below... Alright, old timer, until next time. And tell Sidor: I'll cook a couple more Jellyfish and swing by the village — have some bits and pieces ready for me.
Incoming: \nRighty-o!

Outgoing: \nHey, Vergas, what gives? After your repair job my PDA isn't working at all. Something's off: won't show the time, recipes are gone, messages only send every other time. You looking to get an earful, old man?

Incoming: \nWhy are you barking, son? I'm no programmer, after all. My job is small — charge a battery, wipe the little screen clean of your fingerprints, clean the buttons of canned stew — fixing software problems is a job for a proper person. So, no offense, and, as Sidor's guard likes to say: "To spare you any disappointment, would it be possible to receive payment?"

Outgoing: \nYou're a real piece of work, old man. Alright, I'll bring it by if you tell me who this person is. Probably some egghead. That means I'd have to trek all the way to the Swamps or even Yantar. Docent's place is off the table for me — the Duty guys don't exactly spare bullets when they see me, never mind that they're on the taxpayer's dime.

Incoming: \nWhat's his name, you ask? Blasted memory, it's right on the tip of my tongue but won't come to me... something Musketeer-ish... Aramis, Porthos... ah — Athos. Definitely Athos. He even had one of those fashionable wide-brimmed hats, until he traded it to Robinson for some hair product. Now there's a real programmer, not like me.
Outgoing: \nAre you out of your mind, you senile old galosh? You think I'm an idiot? That lunatic ended up in the Zone when the fence at the mental ward caved in.
Incoming: \nBarking again, son! Your mother will scold you!

Outgoing: \nSorry. I've been on edge since the emission. But the moment that guy powders his nose he starts going on about some vision he had, telling him to go to the Foggy Thicket and extract a philosopher's stone from a "Symbiont" artifact — you know, to turn tin cans into gold. An undertreated alchemist, for crying out loud.

Incoming: \nThat's about right. He organized a whole cult out there. These acolytes wander around the Zone after him, and he's like their high priest. Every night he gathers his flock by the campfire and launches into sermons. And the rabble sit there mouths hanging open, slobbering... hanging on every word.
Outgoing: \nAnd? Any success?

Incoming: \nNot a chance in hell! The things they tried with that "Symbiont" — dunked it in a swamp on Midsummer's night, hammered in bolts pulled off a corpse — only thing they didn't try was smearing it with dung, and all for nothing...
Outgoing: \nAnd where are they doing all this shamanism?

Incoming: \nThey'd have kept dancing in circles till the Greek calends if Father Diodor hadn't broken up that little crowd. He tied their ringleader to the wall in the swamp chapel and drove the demons out of him... along with whatever was left of his mind...
Outgoing: \nI get the feeling there's nobody left to fix my PDA. I'll have to look for a new one.
Incoming: \nWell, my friend, next time you're passing by — drop in. I'll lubricate your device with a bit of vodka. Might help...
Outgoing: \nGuys, turns out they had good reason to throw Sultan out of the Bar!
Incoming: \nHow so?

Outgoing: \nYou've got to watch yourself with that one. I got suckered in — ordered a mini-Dragunov. Spent three days begging Tooth for the recipe, another day cooking the modificate. Burned through half my ammo getting to the stash where the sniper rifle was. And what did I get?.. A peashooter, that's what I'll tell you. Good for scaring rats and nothing else.
Incoming: \nSeriously? It's that bad? Or is someone just a lousy shot?
Outgoing: \nNext time I'm near the Bar I'm rearranging Sultan's face! No, guys — SVD is the only way. That Dragunov can rot in the stash at the Garbage...
Incoming: \nNow, about that stash — let's hear more. We'll give that sniper rifle a fair assessment, heh heh...
Outgoing: \nDon't buy artifact modification recipes from Tooth. All garbage.
Incoming: \nWatch what you're saying!

Outgoing: \nLook: I cooked everything by the recipe, only instead of a Jellyfish I used a Gravi, instead of a Snork leg I threw in a Boar hoof, and I cooked it in a Vortex instead of a Whirligig. Got complete garbage — even Sidor wouldn't buy it!

Incoming: \nAnd when you go up against a Bloodsucker, put on underwear instead of armor, grab a stick instead of a rifle, hold a bolt instead of a knife. And instead of running, walk right up to it puckered for a kiss...
Outgoing: \nHey, what are the army boys looking for at Agroprom again?
Incoming: \nNo idea myself. A few days ago I was watching one of them through binoculars, heard him talking to someone over the radio.
Outgoing: \nProbably someone on the run again.
Incoming: \nDoubtful. This soldier had some kind of device with him. Like a scanner. Who knows what they've cooked up this time.
Outgoing: \nWas he looking for artifacts? Or probing anomalous fields?
Incoming: \nI heard they found a spatial anomaly there — still don't know where it throws you to. Or maybe it's throwing things at them from somewhere.
Outgoing: \nThat's where all the wildlife in that area is coming from. They're pouring out of that anomaly.

Log: \nWhere do these brainless Flesh come from! I spent half a day chasing one green beast all over the Swamps just to get its hide — burned through a ton of ammo! And then the damn thing ran straight into a Whirligig. I swear it did it on purpose so it wouldn't have to give me the hide! Rotten thing.

Log: \n...Tracked down another green Flesh. Decided to shoot out a limb right away so it couldn't run so fast. It still bounds along on three legs, you can't catch the thing! But it tired out quick. Just when I thought I had it, the beast runs straight into a Bloodsucker. One hit — it's done. I barely got away while it was busy feeding. Again without a hide.

Log: \n...Stopped in at the Gut to see the hunters. Asked if you could make a lure that attracts Flesh. Preferably green ones. They looked at me like I was stupid. Suggested I try whistling through two fingers. Comedians.

Log: \n...Can Flesh conspire together? Tracked down another green one. Started the chase, but the cunning beast bolted straight into a Boar herd. While I was fighting them off, hunters showed up at the noise. They dropped the Boars and the Flesh both. Took everything for themselves. Freeloaders. Maybe I should try hunting Pseudodogs instead?

Outgoing: \nI'm at the Army Warehouses. Just watched a Chimera maul a stalker. Near the farmstead by the Freedom base. Dragged the body into a house. The stalker had a great suit on, backpack packed solid too. Who's coming with me to kill the beast? We split all the loot equally.
Incoming: \nDid the Chimera drag off that stalker's gun too? Or did you already help yourself?
Outgoing: \nThe Vintorez? It's lying right next to the house. I'm watching through binoculars — not too keen on walking up alone.
Incoming: \nAlright, wait a bit — I'm nearby, I'll swing over to you. Only the Vintorez is mine, yeah?
Outgoing: \nThat thing's no use to me anyway — it's a jam-happy peashooter. Let's get the job done first, then we'll talk.
Incoming: \nHey.
Outgoing: \nHey yourself.
Incoming: \nYou at the Bar right now?
Outgoing: \nYeah — get some repairs done, rest up for a couple of days.
Incoming: \nWhat's going on over there? Did someone mutate, or did some beast break through the checkpoints and wipe out a bunch of people?
Outgoing: \nWhat? Oh... that... Let me fill you in on the beast-mutant situation. You know Fedka Piston?

Incoming: \nHalf the Zone knows him. You sit down with the guys by the fire, slice up a sausage stick, crack open a bottle to treat the psychic damage... and then this one shows up. Ambles over. Takes off his worn-out boots, hangs his century-old socks on them, and sticks his feet practically into the fire. Airing them out, supposedly. Then he starts up: "They palmed off lousy boots on me again..." Drives me crazy. Buy new boots instead of second-hand junk and stop whining. And the smell above all else — Flesh start sneezing from a kilometer away, forget about any appetite. So he's... forgive me, Lord.

Outgoing: \nI wouldn't say that. Just listen. Fedya decided to head to Yantar. Sakharov threw him a little job — needed five pairs of boots from Snorks for some experiments. So Fedya shot them, pulled the footwear off — and it's not ordinary footwear, as you know. Then our Fedya thinks: why pay even for second-hand boots when there are free ones right here? And in excellent condition. So he picked out a pair in his size, gave them a good clean. For disinfection he soaked them for a couple of hours in a diluted Fruit Punch solution. Then shows them to Sakharov — says, professor, would it be alright if I wore this footwear? Well, Sakharov says it's probably safe. But if he sends him a health report twice a day, he'll also earn ten medical-grade medkits on top of that. And that science needs clinical enthusiasts of exactly this kind.
Incoming: \nAnd?

Outgoing: \nSo he shows up at the Bar in his new kicks. Said he could run for ages without getting tired, jump over fences, and heights didn't bother him anymore — something about the way the boots spring. He walked around the Bar in them for two days, practically didn't take them off. On the third day we're sitting by the fire in the workshop, Fedya starts up again about the boots, and then suddenly he lets out a scream... then leaps from the spot all the way up to the ceiling, then along the walls. Holy mother. And he's screaming — help, ouch ouch... We piled on him somehow, pinned him down, asked what was wrong. Then we look — blood seeping out from under the boots. Tried to take them off — they won't come off. Lucky Fedya passed out. Cut them with a knife — and the boots had sort of put roots into his feet. Horrifying. Somehow we pulled all of it out of his legs. Blood everywhere. Good thing the Swamp Doctor had stopped by the Bar to stock up. He smeared some ointment on it, told him to lie still for three days and get out of the Zone if Fedya wanted to live. That's the whole story.
Incoming: \nWow. Incredible. You know more about this than I do... Hey, I wear a Flesh cloak. What if the same thing happens to me? And by the way — why's his handle Piston?

Outgoing: \nDon't worry about the cloak — there's nowhere left to grow into there, heh heh... They're cured properly by specialists anyway, and Flesh is still a pig, not a former human. As for the handle — he was going on about his boots one too many times, so someone slipped a firecracker or something into them. Everyone found it very funny, except Fedya. Nobody heard a word about boots from him for a week after that.
Incoming: \nHeh. Alright, swing by our little farmstead at the Warehouses tomorrow morning. Got something to discuss, and I don't feel like going it alone.
Outgoing: \nOk. See you then.
Incoming: \n'Sup, broski! Come on, spill the latest. Ain't heard hide nor hair of ya.
Outgoing: \nHey. I got a real sweet deal linin' up. But I ain't tellin' ya shit about it. And let's keep it that way, no hard feelings or any of that bullshit.
Incoming: \nI don't get it, why the hell not?!
Outgoing: \nI don't need any extra ears, wanna keep this on the down-low.

Incoming: \nRight... Now that ain't playin' by the rules! Who lost the cloak for Boryan? Huh?! And who had to give up theirs to cover your ass? Now I'm walkin' around in this trashy jacket like some street punk...
Outgoing: \nDon't start. I'll pay it all back if this works out. Remember how the boss kept dreamin' of a broad, sayin' why ain't there a single female in the whole Zone? Ha-ha, figure that, yeah?
Incoming: \nI don't get it, he needs a broad dragged to him or somethin'? And what kind of deal is this and how's it connected to broads?

Outgoing: \nHe didn't ask to bring nobody, but I already know I'll get respect, honor, and then some. What he needs her for over there, I don't know and don't wanna know. Old pervert... I'm just in it for the cut.
Incoming: \nSo what's the deal? Stop blowin' smoke up my ass!

Outgoing: \nLong story short. I'm headin' from the Garbage to the Cordon. That run turned out real productive - found two sick artifacts and somebody's stash with grub. Ha! The loser hid it under a bus right in the middle of the road, you could see it from a mile away. Might as well have tossed the backpack right on the pavement. Anyway, that ain't what I'm talkin' about. Approaching the gates to the Cordon, I spotted a stalker messin' around with a PDA. Wasn't lookin' around at all, so I took the chance... Overdid it a bit though, splashed blood all over his suit. And I sure could use it right about now.
Incoming: \nSoooo, now this is gettin' interestin'. What stalker?

Outgoing: \nHow the hell should I know? I made too much noise back there and had to make a run for the Cordon. Grabbed the backpack with the PDA and hauled ass. Once I reached the Cordon, decided to dig through it, nobody was around. Climbed the tower near the APC and started clickin' through the damn thing. No stash coordinates, but there was a message history with some Oksana. Well... I'm thinkin', probably found a way to chat with their broads from the mainland. But turns out this stalker was supposed to be her guide here, to the Zone, figure that! And this loser ran straight into me. Got pretty damn lucky. So I'm thinkin', gotta take the chance. Last message said she'll be at the edge of the Swamps in a couple days. What she lost here - I couldn't figure out. PDA just had info on where she'll be. By the way! She promised to dish out some serious cash - double the profit. So that's the deal. Can you imagine what I could score if I bring this lady to Papa Borov? Right. So what? Did I satisfy your curiosity?!
Incoming: \nWhoa. So you decided to play guide and drag her to the Valley? I ain't followin' your train of thought. Spit it out already, you're killin' me!

Outgoing: \nYeah!!! Even a retard would've figured it out by now. The things you do for cash... So yeah, gonna find some decent gear and head out to the Swamps tomorrow mornin'. And then we'll see how the cards fall. Hope I ain't jinxin' it.
Incoming: \nGo on, get it. Just don't shit yourself out there. And yeah! Don't forget my cut, I wouldn't just want my cloak back. Catch my drift?
Outgoing: \nThat's it, I'm done. Ain't wastin' no more time on ya. We'll chat some other time. I'm bailin'.
Incoming: \nShurik, help me out!
Outgoing: \nWhat happened? "Caught a bullet," heh-heh-heh.
Incoming: \nWorse. Caught a "Black Mark"...
Outgoing: \nI told you - no need to check corpses, you half-baked looter.
Incoming: \nYou'd be better off helping a friend. I feel sick enough without you, and I could've gotten a lecture from Lyokha.
Outgoing: \nAlright. There's one way, but it's a matter of conscience.
Incoming: \nI don't give a damn, write it fast!
Outgoing: \nI heard the BM can be given to a person who wants to take it for themselves.
Incoming: \nAnd who would take such luck?

Outgoing: \nWell, that I don't know. Though, when I was at the Garbage, in the hangar - taking some energy drinks from Cook - I heard some guy gave a greenhorn the mark there, and he took it for 20 grand.
Incoming: \nHoly shit! Bucks! 20 grand! Where would I get that kind of money? Spot me at least 5 thousand, huh?
Outgoing: \nNah. Our rubles, of course, electronic ones.
Incoming: \nThat's a different story. I'm right at Agroprom, I'll go hand it over. It's just too hard to live with it. Where are you heading?
Outgoing: \nThinking of heading to the Warehouses, should I wait for you at the Bar?
Incoming: \nDon't wait. Who knows how it will all turn out. You might end up waiting for me forever.
